heading: Furnishing of information, data, or reports by Federal agencies
label: 6307 Furnishing of information, data, or reports by Federal agencies
notes: Amendments2015—Subsec. (b). inserted “or section 6314(b)” after “section 6302(b)(3)(B)” wherever appearing.Pub. L. 114–94

children.heading: .—In General
children.label: a .—In General
children.text: Except as provided in subsection (b), a Federal agency requested to furnish information, data, or reports by the Director under section 6302(b)(3)(B) shall provide the information to the Director.

children.label: A
children.text: make any disclosure in which the data provided by an individual or organization under section 6302(b)(3)(B) or section 6314(b) can be identified;

children.label: B
children.text: use the information provided under section 6302(b)(3)(B) or section 6314(b) for a nonstatistical purpose; or

children.label: C
children.text: permit anyone other than an individual authorized by the Director to examine any individual report provided under section 6302(b)(3)(B) or section 6314(b).

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: No department, bureau, agency, officer, or employee of the United States (except the Director in carrying out this chapter) may require, for any reason, a copy of any report that has been filed under section 6302(b)(3)(B) or section 6314(b) with the Bureau or retained by an individual respondent.

children.label: i
children.text: shall be immune from legal process; and

children.label: ii
children.text: shall not, without the consent of the individual concerned, be admitted as evidence or used for any purpose in any action, suit, or other judicial or administrative proceedings.

children.heading: .—Applicability
children.label: C .—Applicability
children.text: This paragraph shall apply only to reports that permit information concerning an individual or organization to be reasonably determined by direct or indirect means.

children.heading: .—Informing respondent of use of data
children.label: 3 .—Informing respondent of use of data
children.text: If the Bureau is authorized by statute to collect data or information for a nonstatistical purpose, the Director shall clearly distinguish the collection of the data or information, by rule and on the collection instrument, in a manner that informs the respondent who is requested or required to supply the data or information of the nonstatistical purpose.
information that is expressly prohibited by law from being disclosed to another Federal agency; or

information that the agency possessing the information determines could not be disclosed without significantly impairing the discharge of authorities and responsibilities which have been delegated to, or vested by law, in such agency.
